reasoning: The frames show: (1) A blurred overhead facility view; (2) Good Samaritan Hospital Medical Center signage; (3) Medical staff in white coats and navy scrubs; (4) A fitness/rehabilitation device (dumbbell); (5) Laparoscopic equipment/da Vinci robotic console close-up; (6) Healthcare professionals in group settings and interview settings; (7) Trainees at laparoscopic trainers (da Vinci Si and FLS/MISTELS-style boxes); (8) An expert/proctor in discussion with residents. The content is fragmentary and does not show a single coherent FLS task performance from start to completion.

video_assessment: This video is primarily instructional/promotional in nature, featuring interviews with trainees, group educational settings, equipment demonstrations, and facility tours. It does NOT contain a single, complete FLS task performance suitable for formal scoring under the MISTELS rubric. Fragments show setup and equipment, but no timed, formal task execution with clear start/completion endpoints.
cannot_score: Insufficient video evidence of a single complete FLS task performance. No clear timing, no defined task phases, no objective completion criteria visible.
recommendation: This video should NOT be scored as an FLS performance. It is educational/promotional content. If scoring is intended, a video showing one trainee performing one complete FLS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture) with clear task setup, timing, and completion is required.
